riskSummary: GOES-18 stays most credible on orbital safety, but sustainability / end-of-life is the clearest drag on the final score.
summary: NOAA geostationary weather flagship that gives the demo a calm high-altitude comparison target.
demoExplanation: Geostationary orbit with low congestion exposure and a very stable trajectory.

scoreBreakdown.explanation: Each factor contributes up to 25 points. GOES-18 is carried by orbital safety, while sustainability / end-of-life is the main reason it does not rank higher.

factors.label: Orbital safety
factors.explanation: The orbit sits in a geostationary lane that is operationally steady. NASA's sampled arc shows an almost perfectly stable altitude profile. Mission complexity is comparatively contained.

factors.label: Congestion impact
factors.explanation: Geostationary spacing is comparatively orderly in the local model.

factors.label: Debris exposure
factors.explanation: Debris in GEO is long-lived, but conjunction density is still lower than the busiest LEO corridors. The geostationary shell is persistent, but it is not the most collision-saturated regime.

factors.label: Sustainability / end-of-life
factors.explanation: NOAA gets a local stewardship assumption of 5/5. The GEO regime earns a neutral end-of-life adjustment in this heuristic. Mission complexity is scored at 2/5, which changes how much operational slack the model assumes.

dataQuality.label: High
dataQuality.summary: NASA SSC returned 181 source samples from 2026-03-20T12:00:00.000Z to 2026-03-20T15:00:00.000Z. 61 points are kept in the demo trajectory snapshot.

riskSummary: Landsat 8 stays most credible on orbital safety, but congestion impact is the clearest drag on the final score.
summary: USGS-operated public imaging mission used to represent a stable, science-driven sun-synchronous asset.
demoExplanation: Sun-synchronous orbit with moderate congestion exposure and a mostly stable trajectory.

scoreBreakdown.explanation: Each factor contributes up to 25 points. Landsat 8 is carried by orbital safety, while congestion impact is the main reason it does not rank higher.

factors.label: Orbital safety
factors.explanation: The craft flies in a sun-synchronous lane with repeatable geometry and heavy reuse. NASA's sampled arc is stable enough for a hackathon-scale scorecard. Mission complexity is comparatively contained.

factors.label: Congestion impact
factors.explanation: Sun-synchronous shells are structurally busy because many imaging and weather missions reuse them. High-latitude coverage adds more lane crossings.

factors.label: Debris exposure
factors.explanation: Sun-synchronous debris remains persistent because the shell is heavily reused and slowly clears.

factors.label: Sustainability / end-of-life
factors.explanation: USGS gets a local stewardship assumption of 5/5. The SSO regime has a comparatively explainable disposal path in this heuristic. Mission complexity is scored at 2/5, which changes how much operational slack the model assumes.

dataQuality.label: High
dataQuality.summary: NASA SSC returned 181 source samples from 2026-03-20T12:00:00.000Z to 2026-03-20T15:00:00.000Z. 61 points are kept in the demo trajectory snapshot.

riskSummary: ISS stays most credible on sustainability / end-of-life, but congestion impact is the clearest drag on the final score.
summary: NASA-led crewed platform that anchors the demo's busiest LEO lane and highest-complexity operations profile.
demoExplanation: Low orbit with high congestion exposure and a mostly stable trajectory.

scoreBreakdown.explanation: Each factor contributes up to 25 points. ISS is carried by sustainability / end-of-life, while congestion impact is the main reason it does not rank higher.

factors.label: Orbital safety
factors.explanation: The craft remains in low Earth orbit, where maneuver timing matters most. NASA's sampled arc is stable enough for a hackathon-scale scorecard. Mission complexity is high, so the model assumes less operational slack.

factors.label: Congestion impact
factors.explanation: Low Earth orbit carries the highest raw traffic volume in this scorecard. Very low altitude keeps the craft inside the busiest shells.

factors.label: Debris exposure
factors.explanation: LEO debris exposure is driven by dense traffic and frequent conjunction management. The local model applies an extra debris penalty for this lane.

factors.label: Sustainability / end-of-life
factors.explanation: NASA gets a local stewardship assumption of 5/5. The LEO regime has a comparatively explainable disposal path in this heuristic. Mission complexity is scored at 5/5, which changes how much operational slack the model assumes.

dataQuality.label: High
dataQuality.summary: NASA SSC returned 181 source samples from 2026-03-20T12:00:00.000Z to 2026-03-20T15:00:00.000Z. 61 points are kept in the demo trajectory snapshot.
